profile_summary.schema_warning: WARNING: 240 unattributed kernels represent 12.6% of all kernels (exceeds 10% threshold). Wall-time percentages may understate the true cost of layer norm and attention preprocessing ops (triton_per_fused_native_layer_norm_0, triton_poi_fused__scaled_dot_product_efficient_attention_* are the dominant unattributed types). Re-run with --correlation-pass to improve attribution confidence.

architecture_notes: A100 SXM4-80GB (Ampere): 108 SMs, ridge point 156 FLOP/byte (BF16), peak 312 BF16/FP16 TFLOPS (Tensor Core HMMA), peak 19.5 FP32 TFLOPS (SIMT), 2.0 TB/s HBM bandwidth, 40 MB L2 cache. Tensor Core minimum tile: 16x16x16 for BF16/FP16 on Ampere (HMMA). FP8 is NOT supported on Ampere — do not propose FP8. warp_cycles_per_instruction IS available on Ampere but reads null in this profile (smsp__warp_cycles_per_issued_instruction.avg was not included in the ncu metric set). Use eligible_cycles_pct as the latency-bound proxy for this profile. SGEMM dispatch (ampere_sgemm_*_nn) confirms all GEMM operators are running the FP32 SIMT path — Tensor Cores are completely idle for 51.9% of profiled time. Layer deduplication was active: 12 transformer blocks reduced to 2 unique representatives (modules_0 and modules_11); ncu profiled only unique representatives and propagated metrics.
optimization_ceiling: Top 3 operators account for 70.8% of attributed time — strong optimization potential. Top 5 operators account for 71.9%. The single largest opportunity is aten::mm_0 (51.9%): casting model inputs from FP32 to BF16 will route cuBLAS from ampere_sgemm_* (FP32 SIMT, TC idle) to sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_* (Tensor Core HMMA), potentially cutting GEMM time by 4-8x.
